encounter.reason: 3-year well-child examination, persistent asthma diagnosed, inhaled corticosteroid therapy initiated
encounter.location: Triangle Pediatrics, 4201 Lake Boone Trail, Suite 200, Raleigh, NC 27607

screening.name: ASQ-3 (Ages and Stages Questionnaire, Third Edition)

conditions.name: peanut allergy with history of anaphylaxis

medications.name: fluticasone propionate 44 mcg metered-dose inhaler
medications.sig: 1 puff inhaled twice daily via spacer with mask

medications.name: albuterol sulfate 90 mcg metered-dose inhaler
medications.sig: 2 puffs inhaled as needed for wheezing or shortness of breath via spacer

medications.name: epinephrine auto-injector 0.15 mg intramuscular
medications.reason: peanut and tree nut allergy with history of anaphylaxis

medications.name: triamcinolone acetonide 0.1% topical ointment
medications.sig: apply to affected areas twice daily as needed for eczema flares

document_references.description: 3-year well-child visit note documenting interval history of persistent respiratory symptoms, complete physical exam, vital signs, developmental screening (ASQ-3 normal, PHQ-2 score 0), vision and hearing screening (both passed), new diagnosis of mild persistent asthma with initiation of inhaled corticosteroid therapy, review of atopic conditions, and follow-up plan for asthma reassessment in 4–6 weeks.

Written asthma action plan reviewed with caregiver and provided as PDF for family and daycare. Defines green zone (fluticasone BID, no rescue inhaler needed), yellow zone (albuterol every 4 hours, call office if >3 uses in 24 hours), and red zone (albuterol every 20 minutes ×3, call 911 if no improvement).
